task_completion.visual_evidence: The column for table assignments is filled with table numbers for the guests (expected in rows 3 to 66).

task_completion.description: Ensure families are kept together at the same table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Guests with the same family designation (e.g., Smith Family, Johnson Family, Wedding Party) are assigned the same table number.

task_completion.description: Ensure no table exceeds the maximum capacity of 8 guests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The summary table or a visual check of the assignments shows that no table number has a guest count greater than 8.

task_completion.description: Create a summary table for table numbers and guest counts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A summary table is visible (expected at F2:G2) with headers such as 'Table Number' and 'Guest Count', listing the tables and their respective guest totals.

task_completion.description: Use COUNTIF formulas to calculate the total number of guests at each table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows a `=COUNTIF(...)` formula being entered or used in the Guest Count column of the summary table.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet application's GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet program (like LibreOffice Calc) rather than a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually used formulas for the summary table.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows formulas being typed, rather than the agent just typing the final hardcoded numbers into the summary table.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to solve the seating arrangement.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing Python scripts or other programmatic solvers being used to generate the seating plan.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves 64 guests (rows 3-66). Max capacity is 8 per table. There are 13 multi-member families; at least 10 must be kept together (80% cohesion). The Wedding Party has exactly 8 members, so keeping them together will completely fill one table (the verifier expects them at Table 1 exclusively). The summary table must be at F2:G2 with headers like 'Table Number' (F2) and 'Guest Count' (G2), containing at least 3 COUNTIF formulas. Passing requires at least 4 out of 6 criteria met.
